title: Has anyone else automated parts of their trading? How has it affected your results?
author: Typical_Director_214
selftext:
I used to let emotions run my trades and it was costing me a lot.

Now I have a bot that follows my strategy exactly, executes trades automatically, and stops me from making impulsive decisions.

comments.author: Realistic-Falcon4998
comments.body: The biggest challenge with automation is inability to automatically adapt to changes. So you have to set specific rules. It's just like a robot. Nonetheless, it's less riskier than manual trading.

replies.author: Typical_Director_214
replies.body: Agreed. Automation only does what it’s told. I run GenesisGo with strict rules and adjust manually when conditions change. It’s not adaptive, but it helps remove emotional mistakes compared to manual trading.

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
There's a whole new app (and it's the only one in existence lol) that let you actually have a reasoning layer that pre-checks on your rules btw. + all of that is automated off a pre-set trigger that you can setup. 

app is called Cod3x

replies.author: AZ_Crush
replies.body: Can't find it in Google Play

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
Its web based
https://app.cod3x.org/

replies.author: ConcreteBackflips
replies.body: what would Nassim Taleb have to say about this

comments.author: Severe_Waltz_1371
comments.body: Cool — can you share specifics: spot/margin/futures + venue, what data drives entries (candles/order book/news), typical timeframe, and how many trades you average per day/week?

replies.author: Adventurous_Bee_7244
replies.body: i agree

replies.author: Typical_Director_214
replies.body: Spot only using the GenesisGo bot. Candle and indicator based entries, no order book or news. Lower timeframe execution with a few trades per week depending on conditions.

replies.author: Severe_Waltz_1371
replies.body:
Nice — thanks for sharing the specifics. GenesisGo is basically an off-the-shelf, marketing-grade bot, so it’s a different lane than what I’m chasing.  
I’m more into the deeper workshop side of this: designing entry logic from scratch, building an execution engine, and then forging the unglamorous parts — recovery after restarts, diagnostics, invariants, and alerting.  
Honestly, I get at least as much satisfaction from the automation process as from the PnL. Wishing you steady profits and calm charts 🤝

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: bruh, you should check out Cod3x then lol. It's right up your alley. At least for the most part, and all of this is built up to the app layer.

replies.author: gumifan1991
replies.body: Steven!!!!! Seems like we are going to see each other in every AI trading post.

replies.author: Severe_Waltz_1371
replies.body: Nice seeing you guys here 😄 People seem fine trusting “AI” with execution, but get spicy when “AI” joins the discussion.

replies.author: Severe_Waltz_1371
replies.body: Appreciate the suggestion — I’ll take a look 🙂 Quick practical questions though: does Cod3x actually have access to **CME Level 2** data, or is the L2 book purely crypto venues? And on the safety side — who controls the wallet/funds (user-controlled non-custodial wallet vs custodial account / delegated permissions)?

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
There's no CME Level 2 data yet (there will be in the near future), it's purely crypto on Hyperliquid and GMX V2 perpetuals. 

Funds are controlled by your agent's wallet (agent never sees the keys though), non-custodial, air gapped.

replies.author: Patient-Bumblebee
replies.body:
Just use a vibetrading DEX. You can define a prompt that contains all of your logic and have the AI trade it. Its similar to vibecoding but for trading.

Execution is all automated and you dont need to run anything. Data is somewhat limited still but you can still use things like L2 orderbook data, technical indicators and orderflow data inside your prompt.

replies.author: Severe_Waltz_1371
replies.body:
That’s a cool suggestion — I’m genuinely curious. New tech can be either a real edge or just extra risk, so I’m trying to understand the basics before I even look deeper 🙂  
Have you been using it for a while? If you don’t mind me asking: who actually controls the funds (custodial vs user-controlled wallet/contract), where do the prices/spreads come from (and are fills verifiable on a specific venue), and for the L2 order book — is it crypto-native or something like CME futures?

replies.author: Patient-Bumblebee
replies.body:
Its a DEX so entirely non-custodial/self-custodial. 

Prices/spreads are derived from oracles and/or set by MM's.

The order book is part of the DEX itself. There is no consolidated order book like in TradFi.

replies.author: Severe_Waltz_1371
replies.body: Appreciate the clarification — good to know it’s a DEX. I’ll keep watching how it develops, but my entry logic relies on real fill flow from highly liquid venues (Binance), so I’m staying in that lane for now.

replies.author: Plastic_Membership25
replies.body: where can i get this

comments.author: Adventurous_Bee_7244
comments.body: But be careful on risk associated.

replies.author: Typical_Director_214
replies.body: agree on this!

comments.author: Adventurous_Chart360
comments.body:
Try [https://tradebotengine.com/](https://tradebotengine.com/)  
Every recommendation includes a confidence percentage and quality label so you can quickly prioritize entries.

comments.author: StevenVinyl
comments.body:
Yup, my whole setup is automated right now. But not just by a bot lol, that wouldn't be smart. There's a whole reasoning process after a trigger "runs" my strategy using AI.   
Used Grok 4.1 Fast to reason through my strategy, but seems like they broke their tool usage capabilities 2 days ago, now am using mostly Sonnet and Kimi. 

You should try Cod3x, see how it works.

comments.author: gumifan1991
comments.body:
I use a platform called Cod3x which is basically like Cursor or Claude Code for trading. You can use AI for as much or as little as you like - either for research or full strategy automation.

I personally chat with it about trade ideas when I have some free time and have it open positions. I mostly use the automation for risk management.

Works quite well. Team is nice and responsive in Discord. I'd check it out.

comments.author: Traditional_Ear5237
comments.body: Automation is the best man. I traded manually for a year, then I built my automation with ML and trading rules logic. Then a few years later decided to turn this into a company. I mean it is better with automation versus doing it yourself because it is scalable and you can run many positions at the same time. Also there is still a lot of learning involved when you go algo. There are a few very naïve things people do with algo trading which causes them to lose cash. But your odds and your longevity should be much better with automation. So good luck and great job.

replies.author: Typical_Director_214
replies.body: dm dude

replies.author: Traditional_Ear5237
replies.body: Noted
